data.title: The Meteora (MET) airdrop claim has closed, with 9.8% of the total supply remaining unclaimed.
data.description: Meteora's head, Soju, cited data analysis from MeteoraInside on X. The data shows that the MET airdrop claim has closed, with approximately 98.9 million MET (about 9.8% of the total supply) unclaimed. All unclaimed tokens will be transferred to the Meteora ecosystem reserve to support future growth plans.

data.title: Jia Yueting: FFAI completes compliance certification for its first EAI robot, advancing the commercialization path of Web3+ blockchain.
data.description: Jia Yueting released "Dual-List Company Structure Weekly Report 039" on the X platform, disclosing the latest progress of EAI Robotics and the crypto business. He stated that Faraday Future's first EAI robot product has completed US regulatory certification and can be commercialized using a "release = sales = delivery" model, with the goal of completing delivery within the release month. The company is advancing its real-demand-driven EAI robot strategy, integrating Web3 and blockchain technologies, striving to achieve positive profit contributions from humanoid robots in the US market. In the capital markets, BlackRock increased its FFAI holdings to approximately 9.5 million shares in Q4 2025, a sequential increase of approximately 40%, while a Wall Street research institution reiterated its "buy" rating on FFAI with a target price of $5. Furthermore, the crypto flywheel project AIXC launched a global accelerated talent recruitment drive and officially launched the X Space series of events, showing positive initial interaction and participation.

data.title: A South Korean lawyer was convicted of embezzling police settlement funds to speculate in cryptocurrency.
data.description: A lawyer in his 40s who had a cooperation agreement with the Chungcheongbuk-do Police Agency and had long represented police officers in damage compensation lawsuits was convicted of embezzling settlement funds and using them for cryptocurrency investments. On January 24, the Cheongju District Court's criminal judge ruled that the lawyer, in representing three police officers in a civil lawsuit against a suspect accused of obstructing official duties, misappropriated 6 million won in settlement funds paid by the opposing party after the case was settled through court mediation in April 2021. He deposited the money into his personal account for cryptocurrency investments, constituting embezzlement, and was fined 10 million won. The court noted that the defendant's act of embezzling client funds was egregious, but given his confession, remorse, and lack of prior criminal record, the sentencing was appropriate.

data.title: Trend Research withdrew 30 million USDT from Binance to repay loans, and is currently facing a paper loss of $215 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, three hours ago, Trend Research withdrew 30 million USDT from Binance back onto the blockchain to repay loans. Trend Research currently holds a total of 651,300 ETH, worth $1.85 billion, with an average cost of approximately $3,180, and is currently experiencing a paper loss of approximately $215 million. The institution borrowed 1.03 billion USDT on Aave, with a leverage ratio of approximately 2.2x.

data.title: River briefly surged past $79, setting a new all-time high, with a daily gain of over 23%.
data.description: OKX market data shows that RIVER briefly surged past $79, setting a new all-time high, with a daily increase of over 23%, and is currently trading at $73.

data.title: U.S. Dollar Index Dips Below 97 Amid Intervention Speculation
data.description: The U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) fell below the 97 mark on Monday, marking the first time since September of last year. According to ChainCatcher, this decline has sparked discussions among investors about the potential involvement of the United States in Japan's foreign exchange intervention efforts, raising concerns about the dollar's status as a global reserve currency. Daniel Baesa, Senior Vice President at Frontclear, noted that signals of policy coordination could intensify short-term downward pressure on the dollar, particularly if the Federal Reserve adopts a dovish stance.

But last week, that floor cracked in public after Central Huijin Investment, a state-linked fund, sold sold about $67. 5 billion across 14 tech-related ETFs […]

data.title: Brother Machi's 25x leveraged ETH long positions were liquidated again, resulting in a cumulative loss of over $25 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, as the market declined, Machi's 25x leveraged ETH long position was completely liquidated again. Subsequently, Machi (0x020...5872) reopened a 25x leveraged ETH long position, which has now been partially liquidated. His total losses have exceeded $25 million.

data.title: Bitcoin Outperforms Altcoins as Market Favors Leading Cryptocurrency
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Altcoin Season Index currently stands at 27, indicating that Bitcoin has been outperforming most altcoins over the past 90 days. According to NS3.AI, this index evaluates the price performance of the top 100 cryptocurrencies against Bitcoin, excluding stablecoins and wrapped tokens. A score below 75 suggests that the market is currently favoring Bitcoin over altcoins.

data.title: World Liberty Finance Executes Significant Crypto Transaction
data.description: Onchain Lens posted on X that World Liberty Finance has completed a notable cryptocurrency transaction. The organization sold 93.77 Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC), valued at $8.07 million, in exchange for 2,868.4 Ethereum (ETH) at a rate of $2,813 per ETH. The transaction was conducted from the address 0xee7f7f53f0d0c8c56a38e97c5a58e4d321a174dc, as reported by Nansen AI.

data.title: Triple Threats Loom Over Markets Amid U.S.-Canada Trade Tensions and Currency Intervention Plans
data.description: Phyrex posted on X. This week, three significant challenges have emerged that could impact global markets. Firstly, U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened to impose a 100% tariff on Canadian goods, potentially prompting retaliatory measures from Canada and China. Secondly, there are plans for the United States and China to jointly intervene in Japan's currency, which may cause concern among carry trade investors. Lastly, the probability of a U.S. government shutdown on January 31 has risen to 78%, raising fears of a repeat of the October shutdown. These developments are expected to influence U.S. stock futures and the broader stock market upon opening.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Weekend Decline Amid Trade and Economic Concerns
data.description:
Phyrex posted on X. Over the recent weekend, Bitcoin experienced a notable decline, exacerbated by several economic and trade concerns. U.S. President Donald Trump's dissatisfaction with a trade agreement involving Canada and China has been a focal point, with Trump issuing multiple warnings to Canada. His use of tariffs as a strategic tool has raised market apprehensions about potential retaliatory measures from Canada and China, which could lead to increased market volatility.

Additionally, there is speculation about possible U.S.-Japan intervention in the yen's exchange rate, which, while less severe than tariff issues, could impact the arbitrage market, particularly affecting yen-based lending assets. Furthermore, the probability of a U.S. government shutdown has risen, with Kalshi predicting a 78.5% chance due to budgetary issues set to expire on January 31. However, unlike the comprehensive shutdown in October last year, some government departments have secured full-year funding, potentially mitigating the impact.

Bitcoin's turnover rate increased significantly over the weekend, indicating growing market panic amid these negative factors. Investors are facing heightened uncertainty, even before the U.S. stock market opens. The upcoming hours will reveal the reactions of Asian investors, followed by those in the U.S.

The repeated use of tariffs by Trump as a weapon has historically led to unfavorable outcomes, and the market's negative response may prompt further actions. The situation remains tense as stakeholders await developments.

data.title: The Polymarket betting pool for "Gold or ETH to reach $5,000 first" has settled, with total bets totaling nearly $1.62 million.
data.description: According to Polymarket, with international gold prices breaking through $5,000/ounce, the betting pool for "Gold or ETH: Which will first reach $5,000?" has been successfully settled. Ultimately, this betting event attracted nearly $1.62 million. Previously, on January 9th, Polymarket reported that bets on "Gold or ETH: Which will first reach $5,000?" totaled nearly $840,000, with the probability of "Gold reaching $5,000 first" initially reported as 68%, and a deadline of June 30, 2026. If neither reaches $5,000 by then, settlement will be made at a 50%:50% ratio. The gold price was referenced to the COMEX gold continuous contract; the ETH price was referenced to the Binance ETH/USDT price.

data.title: Japan may lift the ban on spot cryptocurrency ETFs in 2028; SBI and Nomura have already begun product preparation.
data.description: Japan's Financial Services Agency (FSA) is studying the possibility of lifting the ban on cryptocurrency ETFs, including Bitcoin, as early as 2028. The plan includes amending the Enforcement Ordinance of the Investment Trust Law to include virtual currencies within the scope of "specific assets" that investment trusts can invest in. Reports indicate that large financial institutions such as SBI Holdings and Nomura Holdings have already begun developing related ETF products. Once approved for listing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange, individual investors could potentially participate in cryptocurrency ETF investments through their securities accounts, similar to trading stocks or gold ETFs. Previous surveys showed that at least six asset management institutions are researching related products, covering both individual and institutional clients. The report also points out that a key prerequisite for lifting the ban is tax reform. Currently, Japan imposes a comprehensive tax of up to 55% on virtual asset gains, and discussions are considering adjusting this to a separate tax system of approximately 20%. Analysts believe that if the tax system and regulations are relaxed simultaneously, it will help expand the asset allocation options for individual and institutional investors. (Nikkei)

data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, World Liberty Finance (WLFI) related addresses have sold 93.77 WBTC (approximately $8.07 million) in exchange for 2868.4 ETH, with an average transaction price of approximately $2813.
